title: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
stakeholder_needs:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholder needs center on: (1) End users—fast, convenient spot-cleaning, light weight (<1.5 kg), adequate runtime (>=20 min), strong suction on common home debris, easy emptying/cleaning, low noise, good ergonomics, and clear status indicators. (2) Purchasers—value, durability, warranty, replaceable/available consumables (filters, battery), and charging convenience. (3) Safety/compliance stakeholders—meeting EU safety standards (e.g., Low Voltage Directive where applicable, EMC, RoHS/REACH), safe Li-ion battery design/charging, temperature limits, ingress protection expectations, and appropriate labeling/instructions. (4) Manufacturing/service—DFM/DFS, parts availability, modularity for repairs, and consistent performance across battery discharge. Derived high-level requirements: Functional—portable handheld vacuum for home spot cleaning; minimum 20 minutes operation on a defined standard mode; effective debris pickup on hard floors and fabrics; bin capacity sufficient for typical quick clean; washable/replaceable filtration; included crevice/brush tools. Non-functional—mass <1.5 kg including battery; ergonomics for one-handed use; noise target; charging time target; battery cycle life; durability (drop/impact) and thermal management; maintain suction over discharge; easy-to-clean pathways to prevent clogs; clear UI (power modes, battery status). Compliance—EU conformity with relevant directives/standards (CE marking), electrical safety, EMC, chemical restrictions, battery transport safety, documentation (DoC, technical file), and user manual warnings. Acceptance criteria need clarification on runtime test conditions, performance metrics (air watts/suction), and exact standards list based on charging architecture and intended markets.
clarification_questions: How should the 20-minute runtime be defined and tested (power mode, nozzle/tool attached, new vs aged battery, and debris load/no-load conditions)?
What minimum cleaning performance targets do you want (e.g., suction/airflow/air watts, pickup efficiency on hard floor vs carpet, and maximum noise level)?

What charging approach is required (USB-C vs dock/brick, removable vs fixed battery), and which EU markets/certifications must be explicitly targeted beyond CE (e.g., specific EN/IEC standards, RoHS/REACH, WEEE/battery labeling)?

table.title: Battery-Powered Handheld Vacuum Cleaner (Home Use) — Requirements/FMEA Action List
rows: The handheld vacuum cleaner SHALL have a total mass ≤ 1.50 kg including installed battery and standard dustbin, measured on a calibrated scale (±5 g).
User fatigue; reduced usability; noncompliance with requirement
Mass budget, BOM control, DFM reviews
Implement mass budget with component targets; select lighter cell format and plastics; remove nonessential features.
Acceptance: 3 consecutive production-representative units measured ≤ 1.50 kg.
Confirm if accessories/tools excluded; requirement assumes standard dustbin installed.
The product SHALL provide runtime ≥ 20 minutes in Standard mode at beginning-of-life, measured with fully charged battery at 23°C ±2°C using IEC 62885-2 derived pickup duty cycle.
User cannot complete typical spot-cleaning; returns/complaints
Insufficient capacity; high current draw; poor motor efficiency; BMS cutoff too early
Cell selection with capacity margin; motor efficiency targets
Runtime test during DVT; BMS log review
Increase pack capacity by ≥15% margin; optimize airflow path and motor control; define Standard mode power ceiling.
Acceptance: mean runtime ≥ 22 min and minimum ≥ 20 min over n=10 units at 23°C.
Clarify runtime cycle definition; align with marketing claims and manual.
The charging system SHALL charge from 0% to 100% state-of-charge in ≤ 4.0 hours at 23°C ±2°C using the specified adapter, without exceeding cell temperature 60°C.
Charge time too long or overheating during charge
Poor user experience; safety risk; cell degradation
Undersized charger; poor thermal design; incorrect charge algorithm
Charger selection; thermal simulation; CC/CV algorithm review
Thermal probe test; charge profile capture
Add thermal sensing on cells; derate current above 45°C; validate adapter output and cable losses.
Acceptance: charge time ≤ 4.0 h and Tmax(cell) ≤ 60°C for n=5 units.
If USB-C PD used, confirm PDO negotiation and compliance tests.
In Standard mode, the vacuum SHALL achieve ≥ 12 kPa sealed suction at the nozzle and ≥ 18 L/s airflow (open flow), measured per IEC 62885-2 methods.
Motor tolerances; fan imbalance; leakage; clogged filter
Supplier motor spec; airflow path sealing design
Tighten sealing specs; add gasket controls; introduce motor incoming inspection and fan balance criteria.
Acceptance: 95th percentile of production units ≥ 12 kPa and ≥ 18 L/s at 50% SoC.
Define test fixture calibration interval and nozzle configuration.
The dustbin SHALL have usable volume ≥ 0.30 L and SHALL withstand 5,000 open/close cycles without latch failure or loss of retention.
Dust spill; user injury risk; product unusable
Plastic fatigue; poor latch geometry; material creep
Redesign latch with higher safety factor; add secondary retention feature; validate with accelerated life test.
Acceptance: no cracks; retention force ≥ 80 N after 5,000 cycles.
Confirm user force limits for opening; avoid pinch points.
The filtration system SHALL achieve ≥ 99.5% particle filtration efficiency at 0.3 µm in the exhaust stream at Standard mode flow, verified on a calibrated particle counter test rig.
Poor seal; wrong filter media; deformation when wet
Seal design; supplier qualification; poka-yoke assembly
Add keyed filter geometry; improve gasket compression; introduce incoming media certification and lot traceability.
Acceptance: efficiency ≥ 99.5% at 0.3 µm for n=3 units per lot.
Confirm whether HEPA class labeling is intended; align claims with EN 1822 if used.
The air path SHALL limit uncontrolled leakage to ≤ 5% of total flow at 10 kPa, measured using differential flow method.
Reduced suction; higher noise; poor efficiency
Add gasket bead; tighten critical tolerances; implement go/no-go seal inspection.
Acceptance: leakage ≤ 5% on 100% of units at end-of-line test.
Define leak test time ≤ 20 s to meet takt time.
The vacuum SHALL produce A-weighted sound pressure level ≤ 78 dBA at 1.0 m distance in Standard mode in a semi-anechoic environment per ISO 3744.
User annoyance; returns; perceived low quality
Optimize fan blade and duct geometry; add isolation mounts; add foam where safe and non-shedding.
Acceptance: SPL ≤ 78 dBA average of 3 units; no tonal peaks > 6 dB above broadband.
Confirm semi-anechoic availability and background noise limits.
During continuous operation in Standard mode for 20 minutes at 35°C ambient, accessible surface temperature SHALL not exceed 48°C (hand contact surfaces) per IEC 60335 guidance.
Insufficient airflow cooling; motor heating; battery heating; insulation buildup
Add thermal cutback in firmware; improve heat spreading; add insulation barriers away from grip area.
Acceptance: Tmax on grip surfaces ≤ 48°C at 35°C ambient, n=3 units.
Define measurement points and contact classification (metal/plastic).
The product SHALL comply with EU Low Voltage Directive 2014/35/EU where applicable and EN 60335-1 + EN 60335-2-2, including dielectric strength and leakage current limits.
Creepage/clearance insufficient; insulation damage; moisture ingress
Perform pre-compliance safety review; design for creepage/clearance; select certified PSU; add protective barriers.
Acceptance: accredited test report pass to EN 60335-1 and -2-2, no deviations.
Confirm whether charging base/adapter brings LVD scope; document rationale in technical file.
The product SHALL meet EMC Directive 2014/30/EU, passing EN 55014-1 emissions and EN 55014-2 immunity without functional degradation beyond self-recovery.
Interference with other devices; noncompliance; shipment block
Motor commutation noise; poor grounding; cable emissions
EMC layout rules; ferrites; shielding; filtering
Add LC filters; motor suppression; improve PCB grounding; validate worst-case cable configurations.
Acceptance: accredited EMC report pass; no user-visible malfunction during immunity (Class B criteria).
Define performance criteria during ESD and EFT (auto-recover ≤ 5 s).
The battery management system SHALL provide overcharge, overdischarge, overcurrent, and short-circuit protection with trip times ≤ 10 ms for external short at the pack terminals.
BMS component failure; firmware bug; sensing error
Redundant protection IC; safe firmware; FMEA
Use dual-level protection (HW protector + FW); validate shunt accuracy; add production calibration and fuse.
Acceptance: short-circuit trip ≤ 10 ms; no venting/flame; pack case temperature ≤ 100°C in test.
Ensure compliance with IEC 62133-2 and UN 38.3 for transport.
The assembled vacuum SHALL survive 10 drops from 1.0 m onto hardwood over concrete (6 faces + 4 edges) with no loss of basic function and no exposure of live parts.
Cracks, battery ejection, or function loss after drop
User injury; product failure; warranty costs
Brittle plastic; weak fasteners; poor battery retention
Material impact spec; mechanical ribs; latch strength
Reinforce corners; add energy-absorbing features; strengthen battery latch; verify with instrumented drop.
Acceptance: post-drop passes suction test within -10% of baseline; no sharp edges; battery remains retained.
Define acceptable cosmetic damage threshold (scratches allowed).
The product SHALL achieve at least IPX1 (dripping water) with no hazardous condition and full recovery after drying, per IEC 60529.
Seal gaps; vent path; assembly defects
Seal design; conformal coating where needed
Add drip shields; improve sealing at seams; specify gasket compression; add corrosion-resistant coatings.
Acceptance: IPX1 test pass; no insulation breakdown; functions pass within 24 h after test.
Confirm whether higher IP rating desired for kitchen/bathroom use.
The product SHALL provide a power button operable with ≤ 12 N force and battery state indication with at least 4 discrete levels, with indicator accuracy ±10% SoC.
Button hard to press or misleading battery indicator
Switch selection; poor debounce; SoC algorithm error
HMI spec; firmware SoC model; component validation
HMI force test; SoC calibration test
Select tactile switch with specified actuation; implement SoC mapping vs load; validate at multiple temperatures.
Acceptance: actuation force 6–12 N; indicator error ≤ ±10% SoC from 10–90% range at 23°C.
Define behavior at low battery: warning at 10% SoC with ≥ 60 s remaining.
The product SHALL achieve ≥ 200 hours cumulative run time without functional failure, and battery SHALL retain ≥ 80% of initial capacity after 300 charge cycles.
High returns; warranty cost; poor reviews
Brushless motor bearing wear; battery degradation; filter clogging
Component derating; life testing plan; supplier PPAP
Implement accelerated life test; select higher grade bearings; define consumable replacement intervals; improve thermal management.
Acceptance: 0/10 units fail before 200 h; capacity retention ≥ 80% after 300 cycles at 1C/0.5C profile.
Clarify warranty assumptions; define pass/fail criteria for “functional failure”.
All homogeneous materials in the product SHALL comply with RoHS 2011/65/EU (incl. (EU)2015/863) and REACH SVHC reporting thresholds (0.1% w/w) with supplier declarations.
Noncompliant plastics/solder; supplier change not communicated
Approved materials list; supplier CoC; change control
Establish material compliance process; require IMDS/IPC-1752A; incoming XRF on high-risk parts.
Acceptance: complete technical file with CoCs; XRF shows below limits on screened parts.
Confirm labeling requirements and user documentation languages for EU markets.

messages.content:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.

Stakeholder needs center on: (1) End users—fast, convenient spot-cleaning, light weight (<1.5 kg), adequate runtime (>=20 min), strong suction on common home debris, easy emptying/cleaning, low noise, good ergonomics, and clear status indicators. (2) Purchasers—value, durability, warranty, replaceable/available consumables (filters, battery), and charging convenience. (3) Safety/compliance stakeholders—meeting EU safety standards (e.g., Low Voltage Directive where applicable, EMC, RoHS/REACH), safe Li-ion battery design/charging, temperature limits, ingress protection expectations, and appropriate labeling/instructions. (4) Manufacturing/service—DFM/DFS, parts availability, modularity for repairs, and consistent performance across battery discharge. Derived high-level requirements: Functional—portable handheld vacuum for home spot cleaning; minimum 20 minutes operation on a defined standard mode; effective debris pickup on hard floors and fabrics; bin capacity sufficient for typical quick clean; washable/replaceable filtration; included crevice/brush tools. Non-functional—mass <1.5 kg including battery; ergonomics for one-handed use; noise target; charging time target; battery cycle life; durability (drop/impact) and thermal management; maintain suction over discharge; easy-to-clean pathways to prevent clogs; clear UI (power modes, battery status). Compliance—EU conformity with relevant directives/standards (CE marking), electrical safety, EMC, chemical restrictions, battery transport safety, documentation (DoC, technical file), and user manual warnings. Acceptance criteria need clarification on runtime test conditions, performance metrics (air watts/suction), and exact standards list based on charging architecture and intended markets.
